questions.question: Explain the difference between SameSite=Strict and SameSite=Lax in preventing CSRF attacks.
expectedPoints: Strict blocks all cross-site requests including top-level navigations
Lax allows safe top-level navigations (GET requests) but blocks cross-site POST requests
Strict provides stronger security but may break user experience
Lax offers a balance between security and functionality

questions.question: Describe a comprehensive CSRF defense strategy that incorporates SameSite cookies alongside other protection mechanisms.
expectedPoints: Use SameSite=Strict or Lax for session cookies as primary defense
Implement CSRF tokens for additional protection, especially for state-changing operations
Use security headers like Referrer-Policy and Origin validation
Employ the double-submit cookie pattern for stateless applications
Conduct regular security testing to identify vulnerabilities
Monitor and analyze traffic for suspicious patterns
